prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `c1990cce`.
codex_response:
Implemented `c1990cce` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: expand the centered source pixel into an `N x N` square, draw a source-colored V from the top center to the mid-side edges, then add blue (`1`) down-right diagonals every fourth parallel diagonal inside and below that V. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint as a consistent starting point; it matched the official examples, so no `notes.md` was needed. No new DSL primitive was required.

The generator keeps the official geometry exactly and varies only the background and V color so the family stays visually faithful while still producing 1000 unique single-row inputs under the ARC `30x30` limit. Verification passed on `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples, and I visually checked a generated preview sheet.
